groups.title: Essentials
groups.description: Product identity, destination, audience, problem, outcome, pricing, and launch objective needed for a usable first result.

groups.title: Positioning & Evidence
groups.description: Feature prioritization, differentiation, use-case clarity, claim accuracy, and product availability wording.

groups.title: Maker Story & Offer
groups.description: Maker comment context, requested feedback, tone, and any real Product Hunt offer.

groups.title: Launch Assets & Controls
groups.description: Launch tags, gallery messaging, demo placement, CTA direction, regulated-content handling, variants, and special requirements.

variables.label: Product name
variables.placeholder: Creatornivo
variables.hint: Enter the public product name exactly as it should appear.

help.what: Provide the product name for this Product Hunt launch.
help.why: The launch package needs a consistent product name across titles, comments, and copy.
help.example: Good example: Creatornivo
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, invented facts, or wording that overstates what the product can do.

variables.label: Product URL
variables.placeholder: https://www.creatornivo.com
variables.hint: Provide the real destination hunters should visit.

help.what: Provide the product url for this Product Hunt launch.
help.why: Product Hunt launch copy must not invent or guess the product link.
help.example: Good example: https://www.creatornivo.com
help.avoid: Avoid fake URLs, unpublished links, or destinations that do not match the launch.

variables.label: What does the product do?
variables.placeholder: A structured AI workspace that helps creators turn briefs into platform-ready content.
variables.hint: Explain the product in plain language.

help.what: Provide the what does the product do? for this Product Hunt launch.
help.why: The package needs a clear factual description before it can create positioning.
help.example: Good example: A structured AI workspace that helps creators turn briefs into platform-ready content.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, invented facts, or wording that overstates what the product can do.

variables.label: Who is it for?
variables.placeholder: Solo founders, marketers, and creators publishing weekly.
variables.hint: Describe the people who should care about the launch.

help.what: Provide the who is it for? for this Product Hunt launch.
help.why: Audience context controls the angle, wording, objections, and CTA.
help.example: Good example: Solo founders, marketers, and creators publishing weekly.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, invented facts, or wording that overstates what the product can do.

variables.label: Problem it solves
variables.placeholder: Blank-page friction and inconsistent prompting slow down content production.
variables.hint: State the main problem or frustration the product addresses.

help.what: Provide the problem it solves for this Product Hunt launch.
help.why: A specific problem makes the Product Hunt messaging useful instead of generic.
help.example: Good example: Blank-page friction and inconsistent prompting slow down content production.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, invented facts, or wording that overstates what the product can do.

variables.label: Main user outcome
variables.placeholder: Create more consistent first drafts without rebuilding every prompt.
variables.hint: Name the realistic outcome users can expect.

help.what: Provide the main user outcome for this Product Hunt launch.
help.why: The outcome anchors benefits without requiring exaggerated claims.
help.example: Good example: Create more consistent first drafts without rebuilding every prompt.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, invented facts, or wording that overstates what the product can do.

variables.label: Pricing type
variables.placeholder: Select pricing type
variables.hint: Choose the pricing model that is currently true.

help.what: Provide the pricing type for this Product Hunt launch.
help.why: Pricing affects positioning, CTA wording, and expectation setting.
help.example: Good example: Freemium
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, invented facts, or wording that overstates what the product can do.

variables.label: Product type
variables.placeholder: Select product type
variables.hint: Choose the closest product category or leave Auto.

help.what: Optional context for the product type field.
help.why: Product type helps the output fit Product Hunt conventions and user expectations.
help.example: Good example: SaaS or web app
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake social proof, fake urgency, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: Primary launch goal
variables.placeholder: Select the launch goal
variables.hint: Choose the main launch objective.

help.what: Optional context for the primary launch goal field.
help.why: The launch goal controls the priority of feedback, awareness, sign-ups, partnerships, or validation.
help.example: Good example: Feedback and early users
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake social proof, fake urgency, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: Output language
variables.placeholder: English
variables.hint: Enter the language or regional variant for the output.

help.what: Optional context for the output language field.
help.why: The launch copy should match the language the maker will publish in.
help.example: Good example: English
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake social proof, fake urgency, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: Key features
variables.placeholder: Template library, saved prompts, generation limits, Markdown export.
variables.hint: List the most important actual product capabilities.

help.what: Optional context for the key features field.
help.why: Features help create concrete copy without inventing product functionality.
help.example: Good example: Template library, saved prompts, generation limits, Markdown export.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake social proof, fake urgency, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: Main use cases
variables.placeholder: LinkedIn posts, newsletters, launch copy, product descriptions.
variables.hint: Describe practical situations where the product is useful.

help.what: Optional context for the main use cases field.
help.why: Use cases make the launch more understandable to Product Hunt readers.
help.example: Good example: LinkedIn posts, newsletters, launch copy, product descriptions.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake social proof, fake urgency, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: What makes it different?
variables.placeholder: Template-first generation with honest limits and no fake social proof.
variables.hint: Explain the real difference from alternatives.

help.what: Optional context for the what makes it different? field.
help.why: Differentiation helps positioning while avoiding unsupported superiority claims.
help.example: Good example: Template-first generation with honest limits and no fake social proof.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake social proof, fake urgency, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: Proof and verified results
variables.placeholder: Use only verified launch facts and product capabilities.
variables.hint: Add only approved metrics, testimonials, customer facts, or evidence.

help.what: Optional context for the proof and verified results field.
help.why: Proof can support credibility, but only when it is real and approved.
help.example: Good example: Use only verified launch facts and product capabilities.
help.avoid: Avoid invented user counts, rankings, reviews, metrics, or customer results.

variables.label: Availability
variables.placeholder: Public beta is available on the website.
variables.hint: Explain who can access the product and any real limitations.

help.what: Optional context for the availability field.
help.why: Availability prevents misleading CTAs for waitlists, private betas, or limited access.
help.example: Good example: Public beta is available on the website.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake social proof, fake urgency, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: Current product status
variables.placeholder: Select product status
variables.hint: Choose the current launch stage.

help.what: Optional context for the current product status field.
help.why: The output must not present a beta, waitlist, or coming-soon product as fully live.
help.example: Good example: Public beta
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake social proof, fake urgency, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: Alternative or competitor context
variables.placeholder: Alternative to rebuilding prompts manually in a general AI chat interface.
variables.hint: Describe fair comparison context without attacking competitors.

help.what: Optional context for the alternative or competitor context field.
help.why: It helps frame the product honestly without unsupported comparison claims.
help.example: Good example: Alternative to rebuilding prompts manually in a general AI chat interface.
help.avoid: Avoid unsupported superiority claims, attacks, or comparison points you cannot verify.

variables.label: Maker or team details
variables.placeholder: Alex, solo founder and builder.
variables.hint: Add the maker names, roles, or team context to mention.

help.what: Optional context for the maker or team details field.
help.why: Maker context makes the first comment and replies sound human and specific.
help.example: Good example: Alex, solo founder and builder.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake social proof, fake urgency, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: Why you created it
variables.placeholder: Built after noticing creators repeating the same prompt setup for every channel.
variables.hint: Explain the motivation or origin story behind the product.

help.what: Optional context for the why you created it field.
help.why: A real maker story helps the launch feel authentic without fabricated drama.
help.example: Good example: Built after noticing creators repeating the same prompt setup for every channel.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake social proof, fake urgency, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: Build journey
variables.placeholder: Started with core templates, then added saved prompts, usage limits, and billing.
variables.hint: Summarize meaningful build milestones or tradeoffs.

help.what: Optional context for the build journey field.
help.why: Build journey details support a credible maker-led first comment.
help.example: Good example: Started with core templates, then added saved prompts, usage limits, and billing.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake social proof, fake urgency, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: Feedback you want
variables.placeholder: Which templates would make your weekly publishing workflow easier?
variables.hint: Ask for specific useful feedback from the Product Hunt community.

help.what: Optional context for the feedback you want field.
help.why: Specific feedback requests work better than generic engagement-bait.
help.example: Good example: Which templates would make your weekly publishing workflow easier?
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake social proof, fake urgency, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: Maker voice
variables.placeholder: Select maker voice
variables.hint: Choose the voice that fits the maker and product.

help.what: Optional context for the maker voice field.
help.why: Tone keeps launch copy aligned with the maker’s natural style.
help.example: Good example: Natural and maker-led
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake social proof, fake urgency, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: Product Hunt offer
variables.placeholder: Off
variables.hint: Turn on only when there is a real launch offer. Choose On or Off.

help.what: Choose whether a real Product Hunt launch offer exists. Offer details appear only when this is On.
help.why: The prompt must not invent discounts, scarcity, codes, or promotional terms.
help.example: Good example: Off
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake social proof, fake urgency, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: Offer details
variables.placeholder: Launch week Pro plan discount for Product Hunt visitors.
variables.hint: Add exact offer terms, dates, codes, and eligibility.

help.what: Optional context for the offer details field.
help.why: Offer details must be precise when a promotion is used.
help.example: Good example: Launch week Pro plan discount for Product Hunt visitors.
help.avoid: Avoid fake scarcity, unclear eligibility, or promotional terms that are not approved.

variables.label: Preferred launch tags
variables.placeholder: Productivity, Marketing, Writing
variables.hint: List Product Hunt topics or tags under consideration.

help.what: Optional context for the preferred launch tags field.
help.why: Tags help suggest launch positioning without pretending Product Hunt approved them.
help.example: Good example: Productivity, Marketing, Writing
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake social proof, fake urgency, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: Gallery assets or ideas
variables.placeholder: Hero screenshot, template picker, generation form, saved content library.
variables.hint: Describe available launch visuals or concepts.

help.what: Optional context for the gallery assets or ideas field.
help.why: Gallery notes guide screenshot captions and visual messaging.
help.example: Good example: Hero screenshot, template picker, generation form, saved content library.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake social proof, fake urgency, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: Demo video URL
variables.placeholder: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=example
variables.hint: Provide a real demo video link if one exists.

help.what: Optional context for the demo video url field.
help.why: The output must not invent demo links or claim a video exists.
help.example: Good example: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=example
help.avoid: Avoid fake URLs, unpublished links, or destinations that do not match the launch.

variables.label: Primary call to action
variables.placeholder: Select primary CTA
variables.hint: Choose the main action for hunters.

help.what: Optional context for the primary call to action field.
help.why: CTA focus keeps the package from asking for too many actions at once.
help.example: Good example: Try it and share feedback
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake social proof, fake urgency, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: Regulated or sensitive area
variables.placeholder: Select only when relevant
variables.hint: Choose a category when the product touches a regulated or high-stakes area.

help.what: Optional context for the regulated or sensitive area field.
help.why: Regulated topics require extra claim caution and may need jurisdiction context.
help.example: Good example: None
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake social proof, fake urgency, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: Relevant jurisdiction
variables.placeholder: United States
variables.hint: Enter the country, region, or market relevant to regulated claims.

help.what: Optional context for the relevant jurisdiction field.
help.why: Jurisdiction can affect safety, legal, financial, medical, or employment wording.
help.example: Good example: United States
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake social proof, fake urgency, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: Number of tagline options
variables.hint: Choose how many tagline options to generate.

help.what: Optional context for the number of tagline options field.
help.why: Variant count controls output size without adding extra fields.
help.example: Good example: 3
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake social proof, fake urgency, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.

variables.label: Additional requirements
variables.placeholder: Avoid fake urgency, fake rankings, or invented user counts.
variables.hint: Add any final constraints that do not fit elsewhere.

help.what: Optional context for the additional requirements field.
help.why: This catches unusual requirements without changing the prompt structure.
help.example: Good example: Avoid fake urgency, fake rankings, or invented user counts.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake social proof, fake urgency, or claims that are not supported by the supplied facts.
